title: IT Transformation Manager Defense (w/m/d)
required_skills: IT strategy and governance (ITIL, TOGAF, SAFe)
bonus_skills: ITIL Expert / SAFe / PMP / PRINCE2 certification
matched_skills: Stakeholder management at C-level — HSBC PMO: C-suite reporting and escalation management
Program governance — HSBC: USD 8M digital transformation budget governance across 6 workstreams
KPI frameworks — HSBC burn-rate dashboards and Hapag-Lloyd KPI reporting
German and English fluency — C1 English, B1 German (as noted in CV)
Project management methodology — agile sprint coordination at Hapag-Lloyd
Digital transformation — supported digital transformation budget governance at HSBC
core_responsibilities: Lead complex IT transformation programs in Bundeswehr, public agencies, and defense industry
Integrate AI-driven, data-driven, and cloud-based solutions in security-critical environments
Develop strategic IT roadmaps and orchestrate transformation programs
Manage change and coach client teams on new technology adoption (AI adoption focus)
Ensure governance, compliance, and KPI standards (VS-NfD, NATO standards)
Build and leverage defense/Bundeswehr network to drive transformation initiatives
recommended_emphasis: HSBC digital transformation budget governance (USD 8M) as evidence of transformation program management
C-suite reporting and risk escalation experience to demonstrate senior stakeholder management
Multi-workstream coordination (6 workstreams) to show program complexity handling
JIRA and Confluence as agile delivery tooling experience
summary: Scoring 28.0, this role is a poor match for Boru Lai. The Defense IT Transformation Manager role at Capgemini demands deep IT architecture knowledge (ITIL/TOGAF/SAFe), defense/public sector domain experience, and AI/cloud technical credentials — all absent from the CV. Boru's programme governance and C-suite reporting strengths are relevant to the programme management dimension but insufficient without the defense domain and IT strategy expertise. Application is not recommended without a significant career pivot or additional qualifications.
